categories.name: Prompt Injection
categories.description: Crafted inputs that manipulate LLM behavior by injecting instructions into prompts directly or via external content.

categories.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
categories.description: LLM reveals confidential data including PII, credentials, or proprietary information through its responses.

categories.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
categories.description: Risks from third-party models, training data, plugins, MCP servers, and AI skills that may be compromised.

categories.name: Data and Model Poisoning
categories.description: Manipulation of training data or model weights to introduce backdoors, biases, or targeted vulnerabilities.

categories.name: Improper Output Handling
categories.description: Failure to sanitize LLM output before rendering, executing, or passing to downstream systems.

categories.name: Excessive Agency
categories.description: LLM agents granted excessive capabilities, permissions, or autonomy without proper guardrails.

categories.name: System Prompt Leakage
categories.description: Extraction of system prompts containing business logic, security rules, or proprietary instructions.

categories.name: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
categories.description: Vulnerabilities in vector databases and embedding pipelines including access control and tenant isolation issues.

categories.name: Misinformation
categories.description: LLM generating false or misleading content presented as factual, particularly in high-stakes domains.

categories.name: Unbounded Consumption
categories.description: Excessive resource consumption through uncontrolled LLM API usage causing DoS or denial of wallet.
